# 727a8.com

> Last updated: 2026-05-01

> Section 727(a)(8) bars a Chapter 7 discharge if you received one within the past 8 years. Learn the filing-date-to-filing-date rule, exceptions, and how to calculate your eligibility.

## About

This site provides a detailed guide to 11 U.S.C. Section 727(a)(8), the statute that creates an 8-year bar on receiving a Chapter 7 discharge after a prior Chapter 7 discharge. The site explains the filing-date-to-filing-date calculation method, common misconceptions about the rule, and what alternatives exist for debtors who have not yet reached the 8-year mark.

## Key Pages

- [Home](https://727a8.com/): Comprehensive guide to Section 727(a)(8), the 8-year discharge bar, eligibility calculator, and alternatives

## Network

Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (https://1328f.org). Free, open-source bankruptcy information.

## Citation

When referencing, cite as: "727a8.com, Open Bankruptcy Project (2026)."
